plan.title: Design project structure and API behavior
plan.task: Define the minimal Node.js project layout for the todo REST API. Confirm the required files are package.json, server.js, server.test.js, and README.md. Decide that server.js should export the Express app for testing and only call app.listen when run directly, while using an in-memory Map and crypto.randomUUID().
plan.rationale: A small upfront design prevents testability and runtime issues.

plan.title: Create package.json
plan.task: Write package.json for a Node.js Express 4 app. Include a start script exactly as "node server.js". Include a test script using Node's built-in test runner, for example "node --test". Add dependencies for express 4 and supertest so the included test can run after npm install.
plan.rationale: The project must be installable and runnable with npm install && npm start.

plan.title: Implement Express app setup
plan.task: In server.js, create an Express 4 app, add express.json() middleware, create an in-memory Map for todos, import randomUUID from crypto, and set a PORT default such as process.env.PORT || 3000. Structure the file so the app can be exported for tests.
plan.rationale: This establishes the runtime foundation required by all endpoints.

plan.title: Implement todo validation helpers
plan.task: In server.js, add helper logic to validate titles. A valid title must be a string whose trimmed length is greater than zero. Ensure invalid create or patch title inputs return HTTP 400 with a JSON error response. Treat omitted title on PATCH as allowed.
plan.rationale: Central validation keeps create and update behavior consistent.

plan.title: Implement create and list endpoints
plan.task: In server.js, implement POST /todos and GET /todos. POST /todos must accept body {title: string, done?: boolean}, reject invalid title with 400, create a todo object {id, title, done, createdAt}, where id uses crypto.randomUUID(), done defaults to false unless a boolean is provided, createdAt is an ISO timestamp, store it in the Map, and return 201 JSON. GET /todos must return all stored todos as a JSON array.
plan.rationale: Create and list are the core happy-path flow for the API.

plan.title: Implement read, update, and delete endpoints
plan.task: In server.js, implement GET /todos/:id, PATCH /todos/:id, and DELETE /todos/:id. GET must return the todo JSON or 404 JSON error if missing. PATCH must return 404 if missing, allow partial updates of title and/or done, reject invalid provided title with 400, update done only when the provided value is boolean, preserve id and createdAt, and return the updated todo JSON. DELETE must return 204 with no body on success or 404 JSON error if missing.
plan.rationale: These routes complete the required CRUD behavior.

plan.title: Add server startup and export
plan.task: Finalize server.js so it exports the Express app via module.exports = app. Add startup logic that calls app.listen(PORT, ...) only when the file is executed directly, using if (require.main === module). Ensure npm start launches the API successfully.
plan.rationale: Exporting the app allows tests while preserving normal startup behavior.

plan.title: Write end-to-end test
plan.task: Create server.test.js using Node's built-in node:test and assert modules plus supertest. Import the Express app from ./server. Write one Jest-style happy-path test using test('POST -> GET -> DELETE todo', async () => { ... }) that posts a todo, asserts 201 and fields id/title/done/createdAt, gets all todos and verifies the created id is present, deletes the todo and asserts 204, then gets that id and asserts 404.
plan.rationale: The requested test verifies the main API flow end-to-end.

plan.title: Write README documentation
plan.task: Create README.md documenting the todo REST API. Include install instructions using npm install, run instructions using npm start, test instructions using npm test, and sample curl commands for POST /todos, GET /todos, GET /todos/:id, PATCH /todos/:id, and DELETE /todos/:id against http://localhost:3000.
plan.rationale: The README makes the project usable and satisfies the requested documentation.

plan.title: Assemble final file output
plan.task: Produce the final answer containing every requested file with exact headers in this format: === FILE: package.json === followed by full package.json content, === FILE: server.js === followed by full server.js content, === FILE: server.test.js === followed by full server.test.js content, and === FILE: README.md === followed by full README.md content. Do not omit any file.
plan.rationale: The user explicitly requested all files with file headers.

result.content:
[router] strategy=always-local → LOCAL (qwen3.6:27b-coding-mxfp8) | conf=1.00 | control: always-local

=== FILE: README.md ===
# Todo REST API

A small Express REST API for a todo list, using in-memory storage.

## Install

```bash
npm install
```

## Run

```bash
npm start
```

The server will start on `http://localhost:3000` (or the port specified by the `PORT` environment variable).

## Test

```bash
npm test
```

## Sample curl Commands

### Create a Todo

```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/todos \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"title": "Buy groceries", "done": false}'
```

### List All Todos

```bash
curl http://localhost:3000/todos
```

### Get a Single Todo

Replace `:id` with the actual todo ID returned from creation.

```bash
curl http://localhost:3000/todos/:id
```

### Update a Todo

```bash
curl -X PATCH http://localhost:3000/todos/:id \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"title": "Buy groceries and milk", "done": true}'
```

### Delete a Todo

```bash
curl -X DELETE http://localhost:3000/todos/:id
```
